content:
Write a fuzzing harness for ONE specific attack surface of `hdf5` (c, address).

Attack surface: High-level Image API (H5IM)
Target API(s): H5IMread_image, H5IMget_image_info, H5IMis_image
Why this surface: H5IM parses image-type datasets with specific attribute conventions. Malformed image metadata (palette, interlace mode) could bypass dimension checks.

This is the first attempt for this surface.

Follow CLAUDE.md for the full workflow (download, write into fuzz-proj, build green via build-project,
write /work/agent/new-harnesses.txt). Exercise the API the way a real caller would — a crash from
harness misuse is a false positive and will be rejected.

#!/bin/bash -eu
# Copyright 2023 Google LLC.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
################################################################################

export LDFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
export CMAKE_C_FLAGS="${CC} ${CFLAGS}"
export CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS="${CXX} ${CXXFLAGS}"

mkdir build-dir
cd build-dir
cmake -G "Unix Makefiles" \
    -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE:STRING=Release \
    -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS:BOOL=OFF \
    -DBUILD_TESTING:BOOL=OFF \
    -DCMAKE_VERBOSE_MAKEFILES:BOOL=ON \
    -DHDF5_BUILD_EXAMPLES:BOOL=OFF \
    -DHDF5_BUILD_TOOLS:BOOL=OFF \
    -DHDF5_ENABLE_SANITIZERS:BOOL=ON \
    -DHDF5_ENABLE_Z_LIB_SUPPORT:BOOL=ON \
    ..

# Make the build verbose for easy logging inspection
cmake --build . --verbose --config Release -j$(nproc)
cd $SRC/hdf5

$CC $CFLAGS  -std=c99 -c \
  -I/src/hdf5/src -I/src/hdf5/build-dir/src -I./src/H5FDsubfiling/ \
  $SRC/h5_read_fuzzer.c
$CXX $CXXFLAGS $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE h5_read_fuzzer.o ./build-dir/bin/libhdf5.a -lz -o $OUT/h5_read_fuzzer

$CC $CFLAGS  -std=c99 -c \
  -I/src/hdf5/src -I/src/hdf5/build-dir/src -I./src/H5FDsubfiling/ \
  $SRC/h5_extended_fuzzer.c
$CXX $CXXFLAGS $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE h5_extended_fuzzer.o ./build-dir/bin/libhdf5.a -lz -o $OUT/h5_extended_fuzzer

zip -j $OUT/h5_extended_fuzzer_seed_corpus.zip $SRC/hdf5/test/testfiles/*.h5

# Build test
mkdir $SRC/hdf5/build-test
cd $SRC/hdf5/build-test
cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE:STRING=Release -DBUILD_TESTING:BOOL=ON ..
cmake --build . --verbose --config Release -j$(nproc)

In the 24 bit RGB color 152 * model example, interlace by plane means all the red components for the 153 * entire dataset are stored first, followed by all the green components, 154 * and then by all the blue components. Attributes conforming to the HDF5 Image and 223 * Palette specification are attached to the dataset, thus 224 * identifying it as a palette. The palette data is of the 225 * type #H5T_NATIVE_UCHAR. 226 * 227 */ 228 H5HL_DLL herr_t H5IMmake_palette(hid_t loc_id, const char *pal_name, const hsize_t *pal_dims, 229 const unsigned char *pal_data); 230 231 /** 232 * -------------------------------------------------------------------------- 233 * \ingroup H5IM 234 * 235 * \brief Attaches a palette to an image. 236 * 237 * \fg_loc_id 238 * \param[in] image_name The name of the dataset to attach the palette to 239 * \param[in] pal_name The name of the palette 240 * 241 * \return \herr_t 242 * 243 * \details H5IMlink_palette() attaches a palette named \p pal_name 244 * to an image specified by \p image_name. export LDFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
export CMAKE_C_FLAGS="${CC} ${CFLAGS}"
export CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS="${CXX} ${CXXFLAGS}"

mkdir build-dir
cd build-dir
cmake -G "Unix Makefiles" \
    -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE:STRING=Release \
    -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS:BOOL=OFF \
    -DBUILD_TESTING:BOOL=OFF \
    -DCMAKE_VERBOSE_MAKEFILES:BOOL=ON \
    -DHDF5_BUILD_EXAMPLES:BOOL=OFF \

    -DHDF5_ENABLE_SANITIZERS:BOOL=ON \
    -DHDF5_ENABLE_Z_LIB_SUPPORT:BOOL=ON \
    ..

# Make the build verbose for easy logging inspection
cmake --build . --verbose --config Release -j$(nproc)
cd $SRC/hdf5

$CC $CFLAGS  -std=c99 -c \
  -I/src/hdf5/src -I/src/hdf5/build-dir/src -I./src/H5FDsubfiling/ \
  $SRC/h5_read_fuzzer.c
$CXX $CXXFLAGS $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE h5_read_fuzzer.o ./build-dir/bin/libhdf5.a -lz -o $OUT/h5_read_fuzzer

$CC $CFLAGS  -std=c99 -c \
  -I/src/hdf5/src -I/src/hdf5/build-dir/src -I./src/H5FDsubfiling/ \
  $SRC/h5_extended_fuzzer.c
$CXX $CXXFLAGS $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE h5_extended_fuzzer.o ./build-dir/bin/libhdf5.a -lz -o $OUT/h5_extended_fuzzer

zip -j $OUT/h5_extended_fuzzer_seed_corpus.zip $SRC/hdf5/test/testfiles/*.h5

# Build test
mkdir $SRC/hdf5/build-test
cd $SRC/hdf5/build-test
cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE:STRING=Release -DBUILD_TESTING:BOOL=ON ..
cmake --build . --verbose --config Release -j$(nproc)

content.text:
Done. Here's a summary of what was built:

**Harness: `h5im_fuzzer`**

The harness treats fuzz input as a raw HDF5 file and exercises the H5IM API in the exact order a real caller would:

1. **`H5IMis_image`** — gate: only proceed for datasets tagged with `CLASS=IMAGE`
2. **`H5IMget_image_info`** — reads INTERLACE\_MODE attribute (via `H5Aread` with the on-disk type, no size guard in the library), plus dimensions and palette count. The harness uses a 1 KB heap buffer for `interlace` — larger than any legitimate value (15 chars), so an overflow from a malformed file implicates the library.
3. **`H5IMread_image`** — allocates exactly `width × height × planes` bytes (capped at 4 MB), then reads. This exercises the dimension-parsing code and the raw `H5Dread` path.
4. **`H5IMget_palette_info` + `H5IMget_palette`** — for each palette reference (up to 8), queries dimensions first, allocates correctly, then reads.

The harness iterates every direct link in the root group via `H5Literate2`, so it works with any dataset name the fuzzer generates.
